raw_items.title: Joan Cusack, the Actress Behind Jessie in ‘Toy Story,’ Takes the Lead
raw_items.summary: After 27 years, the beloved cowgirl doll voiced by Cusack is finally in charge, but the actress played an important role in shaping the character early on.

raw_items.title: Matt Smith Goes Dark on ‘House of the Dragon’
raw_items.summary: “Doctor Who” made him beloved. He channeled that likability into a series of murderers and misanthropes, including in this “Game of Thrones” prequel.

raw_items.title: These Comics Can Barely Muster the Energy for a Joke. That’s Why They’re Funny.
raw_items.summary: Aaron Chen and Dan Mintz specialize in a muted form of stand-up that’s actually quite challenging. But they can get laughs just saying hello.

raw_items.title: To Take Us to the Heavens, James Turrell Went Underground
raw_items.summary: The American artist’s most ambitious “skyspace” installation, opening in Denmark on Friday, invites viewers to connect with the cosmos both inner and outer.

raw_items.title: David Guetta and Sia’s song Titanium got me through my fertility treatment
raw_items.summary:
Hearing their in-your-face banger was a turning point for me – and I’ve never looked back

At the end of 2011, party season was under way but I was in no mood for festivities. Two years into fertility treatment, my body was pumped full of synthetic hormones and felt like a pin cushion, while my head was filled with both the fragile hope of having a baby, and the exhaustion of failed clinical attempts to do so.

raw_items.summary:

raw_items.title: Candice Carty-Williams: ‘People feel very attached to Queenie’

The breakout success of her debut created a publishing scramble for Black writers, but has that appetite for diversity endured? Carty-Williams talks about wanting to quit the TV adaptation, why now is the perfect time for her sequel

raw_items.title: ‘People like me needed Sinéad O’Connor’: how the singer and activist inspired a new dance work
raw_items.summary:
Tony-winning choreographer Sonya Tayeh was ‘broken up’ when she heard about the Irish singer-songwriter’s death three years ago. Now she and a group of over-40s female dancers are paying homage: ‘People love her, people need her’
